concept: Side Effect / Additional Effect Beyond an Intervention's Stated Target
description: An additional effect beyond what an intervention was aimed at, which can turn out useful, neutral, or harmful. Naming something a side effect says only that it arrived alongside the target effect, not what kind of thing it is.
sound_symbolism: Round 'po' starts with a full closure of the lips. Stressed 're' turns the tongue in a balanced middle, and final 'wi' shifts the motion back to rounded lips before rising into bright 'i'. The contour travels across the mouth and ends in a light glide.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha nepha porewi phelu.' (The medicine has a side effect.) State the intervention, effect, timing, evidence, burden, and uncertainty where relevant. Distinct from 'whekaro' (adverse effect), 'whemaki' (allergy), 'phamori' (symptom), 'thirulo' (output), and 'kiphira' (fault).

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A neutral additional-effect term permits benefit and burden to be discussed without minimizing harm or presuming causation.

semantic_domains.cognition: an attributed additional effect with stated uncertainty
semantic_domains.physical: a bodily effect beyond an intervention's stated target
